title: Level 16
wikitext:
{{M.E.G. Theme}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 16: "J. Barry Mental Asylum"}}{{Blurbackground|file=Old-level-16.webp}}__NOTOC__ {{CSS|content= .fandom-community-header__image img { opacity: 0; } .fandom-community-header__image:before { margin-top: 10px; position: absolute; content: ''; background: url('https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/backrooms/images/a/af/16shit.png/revision/latest?cb=20260625160559') no-repeat no-repeat; width: 100px; height: 110px; background-size: 90px 105px; } }} {{Collapse|num=1|initial=
The Asylum Service:
After three days of wandering without food or sleep, the asylum looked like a miracle. The lobby was spotless. Soft music drifted from hidden speakers, warm lights chased away the darkness, and a polite receptionist greeted me with a smile that never seemed to fade. She handed me an old-fashioned brass key engraved with the number 416. '''"Enjoy your stay. Check-out is available whenever you're ready."''''' The padded cell was perfect. The bed was soft, and a full meal sat waiting on the table. For the first time since entering the Backrooms, I felt safe. I told myself I'd only stay for one night. Morning never came. The clock beside the bed still read 8:00, though I was certain I had slept for hours. When I stepped into the hallway, every door looked identical, stretching farther than before. I tried finding [[Level 0]] again. Instead, I found another hallway. And another. Every few minutes, I passed a maid pushing a cart. She smiled politely each time we crossed paths. '''"I hope you're enjoying your stay."''''' Eventually, I reached the elevator. I pressed the button for the ground floor. The doors opened onto another hotel corridor. I laughed at first, thinking it was some strange trick of the Backrooms. Hours later, I wasn't laughing anymore. Every staircase, every elevator, every emergency exit led deeper into the hotel. Days passed. The meals kept appearing. The towels were always replaced. The bed was always made before I returned. It was as if the hotel wanted nothing more than to keep its guests comfortable. One evening, I returned to my room and noticed something different. A framed certificate hung above the desk. "Congratulations on your 10th Year as Our Valued Guest." I tore it from the wall. Behind it was another. "25 Years." Then another. "50 Years." The room had been prepared for me long before I arrived. As I stumbled back into the hallway, I saw the receptionist waiting at the end of the corridor, still wearing the same pleasant smile. '''"Would you like to extend your stay?"''''' For a moment... I almost said yes.
|final={{Collapse|num=168|initial=[[File:16Photo.png|center|thumb|700x700px]]
|final=

Audio taken from a drone on '''Level 16'''.
{{Underline|6F754E}} {{Image Banner|image=Old-level-16.webp|caption= }} {{Class 2|color-h=123|color-s=39%|color-l=33%}}
'''Level 16''' is the 17th level of [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]].
='''
> Description:
'''=
{{Blurimage Border|file=Lvl16.jpeg|𝚏𝚒𝚐=1.0|caption=⁣
A photograph of '''Level 16's''' hallway intersection shows a cross on one of the walls and a line of lockers on another.
|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil}} "Level 16," also called "J. Barry Mental Asylum," is a defunct psychiatric facility that stretches on without clear limits, endlessly repeating itself. As one moves farther from the starting point, the asylum begins to copy the layout of the main building, seamlessly connecting to new sections that mirror or closely resemble the areas previously passed. Despite the facility having infinite navigation, it appears relatively grounded, much like a real mental asylum. Wanderers always arrive outside the facility, where it is perpetually always daytime (most likely the morning or afternoon). The exterior resembles a busy, fully operational asylum entrance—brown brick walls, tan and green accents, and numerous glass windows of varying sizes, some offering glimpses into the interior. From a distance, finer architectural details become indistinct, as if the building refuses to render fully.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=If you are reading this, chances are the first thing you're looking for is an exit. If so, then lucky you, I guess. I'm just a lost case now in this mental asylum that you all call "Level 16". So, if you ever find an exit, you're full of luck. If not, you're going to join the lost causes.|image=Lvl16.jpeg}}
Despite the illusion of activity, no people or entities exist outside. Ambient sounds—birds, distant cars, and occasional machinery—create a strangely comforting, grounded atmosphere. The only accessible outdoor areas are the parking lot and the grass fields surrounding the building. These fields contain countless sunflowers, dandelions, six tall oak trees, and clusters of shrubs and bushes. The parking lot features streetlights, abandoned wheelchairs in designated spaces, and several gray and blue cars from the early 2000s. Each car has a unique license plate and a barcode referencing various Backrooms levels, though none function as transportation. A set of double doors leads into the asylum’s interior, beginning in a basement‑like area filled with dust and scattered leaves. Sunlight filters through the basement windows, yet the view outside suggests the wanderer is somehow on the "second or third floor," overlooking more trees, grass, and fenced-off grounds. Continuing forward leads to a staircase ascending into the next small section—lit by bluish‑green lights and lined with blue lockers, waiting couches, chairs, paintings, candles on desks, and occasional wall-mounted crosses. The surrounding environment is eerily dark, relying on daylight or sunlight from windows or exit signs to illuminate traversal through the level. This part of the level has no decay, except for occasionally dusty floors or vines sprouting near one of the windows in dark hallways. Blue lockers and hospital room doors occasionally appear alongside the walls in this section for at least 50 miles. There are clock-ticking noises and rolling hospital beds idly placed near some doors or hallways, but not most. The exit signs in the hallways seem to have these greenish or reddish lights in the darkness. The hallways with windows that let in more sunlight seem to have even more waiting areas with places to sit; there are corkboards covered with Post-it notes and documents about Level 16, and subtle poster advertisements about furniture, medicine, or doctor's visits.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=At first, this place feels like a blessing. Warm beds, hot meals, quiet halls... everything you've wanted since entering the Backrooms. That's how it traps you. The hotel doesn't lock its doors—it makes you forget why you wanted to leave in the first place.|image=Old-level-16.webp|image-align=center}}
[[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]] and [[Entity 9|facelings]] wander the hallways of these sections, with [[Phenomenon 35|wretches]] themselves being more dangerous. Eerie sounds of [[Phenomenon 35|wretches]] can be heard inside rooms, behind certain doors that are half closed. These sounds can cause wanderers to experience perceptual instability. They usually occur in dark hallways where it's usually hard to see unless people have a light source. Staying near the doors where the sounds come from may provoke the [[Phenomenon 35|Wretch]] in the room to attack them in the hallway, and when they do attack, it's hard to see them even if people have a quality light source. It's highly recommended to listen for the sounds, but not to stay in the area for more than 10-15 seconds. The [[M.E.G.]] has established four division categories for "Level 16: Central Zone, Recursive Zone, Emulation Zone, and Novelty Zone''', with the main outpost at the center. '''The Central Zone''' serves as the entry point for the level. Since arrivals typically began here, settlements and outposts were quickly established, making it common to encounter others in this part of the asylum. One may contemplate departing the Central Zone and advancing to the Recursive Zone by traveling one mile in a single direction from their starting position. Recursive Zones exhibit construction patterns remarkably similar to those of the Central Zone, yet they use comparable furniture, appliances, and architectural blueprints. The critical difference is that no colonies or outposts exist in this zone because more irregular individuals travel through it. {{Blurimage Border|file=Old-level-16.webp|𝚏𝚒𝚐=2.0|caption=
The first known photograph of '''Level 16''''s hallway.
|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil}} This zone is often an exploration area for more assets, notably hospital equipment and medical supplies. Despite this, there are still noticeable differences from the prior site, such as cleaner surroundings and a greater sense of space. If one traverses further—approximately two to three miles—one will ascend into the Novelty Zone, where the surroundings and furnishings are more cutting-edge, with most furniture and items equivalent to those prevalent in modern times. The architectural layout is more innovative, and individuals may find that the building features multiple floors and a larger floor area. The Novelty Zone is the final segment of "Level 16," located deep within the level in an outlying spot that requires a lengthy, uncertain approach. Few travelers have proceeded into this section because many wanderers continually evacuate the level without acknowledging or undergoing harsh mental threats. Witnesses have observed that the mental asylum appears futuristic, much like [[Level 15]], suggesting that the Novelty Zone's building may be a replica of a distant future. As a result of numerous individuals consistently leaving the level when they attain a certain point, it is still up for dispute to determine if someone manages to achieve '''Level 16's '''deepest portion or whether there are further zones away from this. {{Blurimage Border|file=Level16-4.png|𝚏𝚒𝚐=3.0|caption=⁣
A Novelty Zone corridor with hospital equipment; a traveler can be seen at the corridor's end.
|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil}} Ascending the level, one finds oneself in the mental institution's passageway, with the formation assembled entirely of concrete and painted blue, white, or a combination. The corridor was virtually spotless in every respect, including the concrete flooring, which retained a smooth, greasy quality that suggested a recent cleaning. On either side of the hallway are several doors with blank signs opening into hospital rooms. Other rooms serving different purposes, such as therapy rooms, medication delivery areas, or staff stations, feature windows that allow views of the outside. A few items, including paintings, announcement boards, and lockers, are organized in a pale, uncomplicated manner in particular hallways. If one manipulates the locks to access unfrequented lockers, one may discover food containers, almond water, medical supplies, and other valuable items. Multiple emergency equipment items, including fire exit signs, are installed at many hallway intersections; tracking the exit sign direction will eventually guide one to the building's front entry or exit door, as many are confident that construction gateways will lead one to another duplicate mental asylum building. The same cruise approach may apply to adjacent structures by following the same path as the previous building; nonetheless, considering some buildings have layouts that differ significantly from others, the repeated traversing pattern won't function accurately. furniture, medicine, or doctor's visits.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=I've met people who've lived here for days, months... even years. They all said they'd leave tomorrow. Tomorrow never came. If someone offers you another night's stay, remember this: comfort is the strongest prison Level 16 has to offer..|image=Level16-4.png|image-align=center}}
What lies within a hospital room is frequently vacant and abandoned, unlike the corridor, which remains pristine by unexplained means. The only furnishings in the room are outdated mattresses and a bathroom; the space's overall disarray suggests the chamber was once occupied but abandoned for an extended period. The room's general appearance gives no indication of cleanliness or supervision, with moss growing around the corners and the wallpaper pulled away and decomposing, revealing the soundproof wall beneath. Entering the chamber should be done cautiously, as any movement might stir up the thick layer of dust that covers the entire space, making breathing difficult for anyone nearby. Plenty of the rooms feature windows, illuminating the area with a white void due to the room's lighting fixtures malfunctioning. Windows are also visible on the hallway wall, brightening a dark vacuum rather than the rooms inside; attempts to approach the windows have so far been thwarted by the indestructible glass, while many people believe the windows in '''Level 16''' are entities. A few wheelchairs, medical beds, steel rolling tables, and other equipment could be inside some rooms; several might block the entrance and require forced entry. '''Level 16's''' ventilation, heating, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems are anticipated to be in ancient condition and used in bygone eras similar to the building itself; they remain operational effectively while seemingly needing maintenance or overhaul. Every hallway corner includes vents covered in steel panels. Many individuals have reported reglowceiving chilly air from the vent, calling into question whether the gap's track leads. All attempts to locate the opening's origin have been ineffective due to the narrow opening measurement and the impenetrable steel panel. Close inspection reveals extensive dust buildup filling the vent's interior and minimal exhaust fan movement, indicating prolonged use. The most prevalent cooling system in many locations is window air conditioning; these units are relatively old and seem unable to deliver a sufficiently comfortable level of cooling because they are designed for smaller spaces. Regardless, the average temperature of Level 16 remains at 21°C and may descend as low as 12°C. Many individuals speculate that the rationale for this abnormally cold temperature doesn't stem from the place's airflow system but rather from its bizarre properties. Many mental institutions maintain communication systems. Wall phones are located at specific hallway intersections and in areas such as the lobby, staff room, and cafeteria. The phone can only contact other adjacent phones by pressing the button, and dialing each number will reach different phones across the building. The mental institution occupies eight levels, including the basement, all of which are accessible either by stairways or by the building's fire evacuation stairs. Because one always arrives at the ground floor when entering the building, the higher floors tend to have fewer people than the lower floors. Reaching the stairwell's lowest level will lead to a steel-reinforced doorway; the opening will take one underground. The mental asylum's basement is a group of rooms with numerous cables and wires connected to generators, presumably the building's electrical source, which occupy various spots throughout the building. Wires and cables were deteriorating, exhibiting various signs of damage, and required restoration or replacement, as they may leak and be detrimental at high voltage levels; one ought to remain a safe distance from the multiple wires or avoid the basement. Several rooms may feature water heaters and boiling machines linked to an extensive network of pipes, allegedly for integration into all institutional restrooms and hospital bathrooms. Unlike cords and wiring, pipelines don't leak or drip liquid, even when they are in disrepair and might rupture at any time—unless someone attempts to destroy one. Nevertheless, pipes can be hot because of the hot water they contain, so one should avoid touching them. Almost the entire building, including restrooms and hospital rooms, has access to a water supply system whose water is clean and sufficient for washing and cleaning, although it may contain some bacteria, making it inappropriate for drinking from the tap or from the pipe. {{Blurimage Border|file=Level 16o.webp|𝚏𝚒𝚐=4.0|caption=An insulator was found inside the basement, which also contained a typical wheelchair.|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil}} The hallway path may lead to an enormous area, supposedly the reception, with a front entrance to another building on one side of the chamber, each building providing its lobby room on the ground floor. Following the exit sign leading to the front entrance will bring one to this portion, or entering the level may initially take one to the lobby. The space has a few seats and tables in the corner and an empty reception table against one wall. [[M.E.G.]] imposed free public WiFi inside a few of the receptions in the Central Zone. The network is called "JBarryAsylumGuest," and the password is 3312003. A steel-reinforced door with a staff-only notice likely marks the entrance to the break room, also known as the staff room. The break room features a large table and many seats in the center. One side of the room has an empty vending machine—though it can accept commodities—and a table with various electrical appliances and kitchen equipment, such as a kettle, oven, blender, coffee machine, microwave, and others. Break rooms correspond to vacant chambers made of barren cardboard or wood that were previously used for storage. Since travelers and several outposts often establish this location as their headquarters, visitors frequently use the break room as a meeting spot.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=The receptionist always smiles. The rooms are always clean. The lights never burn out. Nothing ever changes here... except the guests. One day you'll realize you've forgotten what the outside halls looked like. That's when Level 16 knows you've checked in for good.|image=Level 16o.webp|image-align=center}}
Because a group of people routinely dwell or remain within the room, [[M.E.G.]] also formed a network called "JBarryAsylumStaffMember" that does not require a password. Aside from the reception, the corridor might lead to the canteen, which resembles a break room but is larger and includes plenty of seats and tables. The room's corner has large tables with many buffet pans for an enormous amount of food. Many outposts utilize this location to cook and store food and drinks. '''Level 16''' has a significant mental risk and a high potential for developing cognitive distortion. Dieting, dehydration, and absence of sleep can increase the risk of mental hazards, as it is not probable to occur in travelers with good physical and cognitive health. Travelers with a history of depression, mental illness, or medical conditions are also more likely to suffer from this risk. When individuals are susceptible to hallucinatory shifts, they may experience delusions that involve believing they are in an reality displacement. {{Blurimage Border|file=OIP (2).webp|𝚏𝚒𝚐=5.0|caption=
An image of Level 16's garage.
|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil}} They may also develop paranoia and perceive numerous beings and entities endeavoring to harm them. They'll start to lose focus and get distracted, unable to concentrate. Victims also report that when they blink or lose focus, their surroundings turn upside down or blur, and the spot where they stand shifts to another location. Whichever mental illness is driving them, they can provoke severe sanity loss. If one begins to exhibit symptoms of mental instability within the level, one should immediately swallow almond water. While it may not momentarily alleviate the severity of the effects, it can lessen some of them. One should find a peaceful space to rest, conserve energy, and maintain well-being. Nowadays, '''Level 16''' has decent food and drink, along with numerous people and outposts existing within the level. The chances of a person confronting these mental hazards are much lower than in earlier times, and it wasn't an overwhelming reason to be concerned.
The Missing Institute:
[[File:Level16.webp|400px|link=]]

'''Level 16' was named after the mental institute because [[M.E.G.]] believed it was associated with the mental asylum "Clement Memorial Mental Institution," also known as "J. Barry Mental Asylum," whose name originated with the mental asylum's owner. J. Barry Mental Asylum was a tiny non-government institution for the mentally ill that opened in 1969 on the outskirts of Newberry, Michigan. However, because the institution is located in a remote area and isn't widely recognized, virtually nothing is publicly known or understood about its history. There were 117 staff members and 1,542 patients hospitalized in total; most patients were not well known or of low social standing. The discovered blueprint and the interior of '''Level 16' have an exact resemblance to Clement Memorial Mental Institution.
[[File:Level16-1.webp|400px|link=]]

On September 16th, 1988, The mental asylum had vanished, but no prominent statement about the institution's missing case has been stated or raised. People now have been neglected and are unaware of the incident or the facility's existence, as they have forgotten. Remarkably, neither the local government nor any other governmental organization knows about the hospital's disappearance, despite a document from the government of the vicinity claiming that it stood on unstable terrain above a cave system and had fallen into a sinkhole. One eyewitness has been around for the entire occurrence, but it doesn't seem like anyone ever believes him. He asserted that the hospital "simply fell through the ground, as if there was nothing underneath it" and that the only remnants remaining were electrical wiring, chunks of concrete, and piping protruding from the ground. He also claimed that there was no known cave system beneath the hospital's location; as a result, the remains were excavated from the ground and have since become an empty landscape of flat glass.
[[File:Level16-2.webp|400px|link=]]

The whole asylum noclipped into the Backrooms, becoming "Level 16," for no apparent reason, along with every patient and staff member within. Employees who survived the tragedy claim that as the hospital disappeared, it seemed as though something had struck the ground severely, injuring a few employees. When they attempted to leave the mental asylum, they realized the area outside the institution was a void. All communication systems and Wi-Fi networks were inoperable and had vanished, making it impossible to seek or contact assistance. They soon saw that all patients had no faces and transferred to Facelings, who became more hostile and attacked staff members in any manner. They attempted to confine patient Facelings within the room, and some Facelings successfully broke out, resulting in an altercation in which all staff members had to employ every weapon available in the hospital to fend off the Facelings. Following the incident, several employees died, eliminating multiple Facelings. While many hospital staff members died from malnourishment and suicide, many more perished from a shortage of food and water due to the institution's limited supplies. After a few months, some employees noticed that other medical buildings appeared and connected to their facility. Fortunately, more resources, especially food and drink, emerge within these mental asylum buildings, allowing staff members to live; nevertheless, few Facelings remain inside the structure, which resulted in a few individuals going missing. The location then spread to an immense size as new buildings started popping up and linking to others. On March 31st, 2003, an anonymous person discovered the place. Many travelers began to visit the location until [[M.E.G.]] named this location Level 16 of the Backrooms. Although many are traumatized by this happening and believe it is "God's punishment" for their heinous crimes against the patients they mistreated, most are unaware of the Backrooms. Only 11 staff members reside inside "Level 16," with 3 leaving the level. The remaining staff members formed their outpost at the break room with the assistance of M.E.G, and many people dubbed this group "The Faced Ones" because of the long time they were stuck in there, fighting and facing events that endangered their lives.
='''
> Entities:
'''=
[[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]] are the most common entities throughout the level, sometimes followed by [[Entity 9|Facelings]]. They usually wander through hallways that have minimal light and remain dormant in padded rooms, with the door shut or half closed. They do wander into modern hallway areas with the most light, but only if they can access it, and this is somewhat rare. The other type of entity that can appear in this level is [[Entity 2|Windows]] which can act or pretend to be one of the interior's windows. It's unknown how many [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]], [[Entity 9|Facelings]], and [[Entity 2|Windows]] in total there are in Level 16, but it isn't an overwhelming amount where travel is impossible to map out.
='''
> Colonies & Outposts:
'''=
===>> M.E.G Base "Asylum Archivers":=== M.E.G. Base "Asylum Archivers" is a small M.E.G outpost consisting of about 30 members situated in two break rooms that link together, considering their outpost to be the hub of Level 16. A break room provides a place for [[M.E.G.]] members to rest, while another room serves for documenting and storing a large number of documents, paper reports, and data related to this level. The group will continue to research the level and attempt to explore the level's deep area to determine more information. Document data storage is somewhat secure. The group is willing to exchange, especially for hospital equipment. ===>> The Faced Ones:=== "The Faced Ones" is a group of 11 staff members who managed to remain and live inside the level after Level 16 collapsed into the Backrooms. They established their base near the [[M.E.G.]] outpost in the spacious break room. The group accepts patients to treat ailments or any sickness if they are travelers. For their protection, they frequently hoard weapons—typically tasers. No traveler will be permitted to join the group. ===>> Uni Citadel Outpost "Hospital"=== A Uni Citadel Department of Exploration team of around thirty people administers the outpost. Three members transport supplies to the outpost every month. The base resides in a cafeteria where a significant quantity of food, drinks, and supplies for exchange and trade with other travelers, predominantly for healthcare equipment, are delivered to their outpost on another level. The outpost often transacts business with B.N.T.G., their primary exchange client.
='''
> Entrances & Exits:
'''=
{{Blurimage Border|file=BrightLevel16.jpeg|𝚏𝚒𝚐=6.0|caption=
A rare corridor possessing functional lighting.
|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil}} ===>> Entrances:=== * Traveling too far in [[Level 14]] may lead one here. * Finding anesthesia in [[Level 14.1]] and taking it will transport one here. * Going through a hospital door in [[Level 15]] will take one here. * A particular building resembling an asylum in the Outskirts of [[Level 32]] will take one here. * An asylum in [[Level 41]] will take one here. * Sleeping at nighttime in [[Level 123]] may rarely bring one here. * Tying up one with a thread in [[Level 256]] will take one to here. * Exiting through a hidden vent in the exterior building walls of [[Level 405]] will take one here. * Some houses in [[Level 974]] will lead one to here. ===>> Exits:=== * Exceedingly dark and cramped areas lead to [[Level 0.7]]. * A door difficult to open located deep into the hospital's basement will lead one to [[Level 3]]. * Going through a medical office door may lead one to [[Level 4]]. * Random doors may lead to [[Level 14]]. * Various factors may cause the hallways to fade into [[Level 17]]. * Pressing the buttons "9" and "8" on the vending machine in the break room will transport one to [[Level 98]]. * Severe cognitive collapse or sudden lose of body control may cause one to awaken in [[Level 144]]. * Following shifting retrofuturistic passages in the Novelty Zone causes the asylum’s architecture to dissolve, depositing the wanderer into [[Level 400]]. * Finding a wheelchair moving on its own is a sign one is going to be teleported to [[Level 223]]. * Toying with hospital equipment might take one to [[Level 268]]. * If a wanderer attempts to escape through a broken window during a distortion event, they may fall into [[Level 777]]. * Finding a hidden light blue 1970s Cadillac with a photograph on the windshield of an advertisement to a motel in the parking lot of the level will transport one to [[Level 69]]. ----
